title: aspidium cochleatum
intro: Aspidium cochleatum, also known as maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to North America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues such as arthritis, rheumatism, and skin conditions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits also include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems, and promoting wound healing. Aspidium cochleatum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine.
